title: eremophila shonae subsp. shonae
intro: Eremophila shonae subsp. shonae is a shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Scrophulariaceae, also known as the figwort family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with the roots and leaves being rich in alkaloids, particularly eremophila alkaloids, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Eremophila shonae subsp. shonae include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions, wounds, and fever. Eremophila shonae subsp. shonae is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, which can be used externally to treat skin conditions or internally to aid in respiratory relief.
